Another man helps her put the contact lenses in.

sentences: Made by the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), the 1976 film, “Operation Breakthrough Equal Opportunity Housing,” profiles the United States’ first large-scale, unprecedented industrialized housing program. The opening sequence opens with an up-tempo theme, and we’re shown several images of tall residential and business buildings, crowded sidewalks, and streets jam-packed with cars. At mark  the commentator lists the various factors that limited housing production during the 1960’s, including expensive land and building costs, financing requirements, transportation costs and the need for skilled craftsmen. At mark 03:57 we’re shown on a map the 218 prototype construction sites proposed by government officials. These sites were chosen to showcase the innovative housing methods and materials that would be used. Nine sites were ultimately chosen: Indianapolis, IN; Kalamazoo, MI; King County, WA; Jersey City, NJ; Macon, GA; Memphis, TN; Sacramento, CA; Seattle, WA; and St. Louis, MO

At mark  we’re taken inside a housing systems assembly plant where we see housing producers building and completing the housing units. Pre-assembled wirings, installation, and plumbing, are shown at mark 05:20. “Regardless of the materials used: concrete, wood, metals, plastics, the completed units are strong and durable,” the narrator reminds us at mark 05:31. At mark 6:56 we’re shown a red oversized shipment truck, and a freight train used to transport the completed units to its sites. And at mark 07:15, workers are seen erecting several different units. At mark 08:20, teams of landscape architects and planners are seen at some of the demonstration sites planning and developing concepts. The film comes to a close as an on-screen collage shows happy families of all ages and ethnicities enjoying their Operation Breakthrough constructed homes

Event: A video explains the symptoms of water hemlock poisoning.
	•	Actions & Objects: A man eats poisonous flowers and subsequently falls to the ground; medical professionals then provide care in an intensive care unit. The video displays a brain, labeled right and left hemisphere; and an image of water hemlock, with a woman holding her head in confusion. Visuals then present dizziness, confusion, abdominal pain, shaking, nausea, vomiting, drowsiness, and weakness as symptoms. Next shown are medical professionals working, followed by visuals of kidneys, blood in a petri dish, and a diseased brain. There are also images representing muscle and nerve interaction, tingling, skin numbness, hallucinations, dilated pupils, cardiovascular symptoms, low oxygen, high carbon dioxide levels, ventricular fibrillation, death, lung illustrations, diagrams of the heart, and retrograde amnesia definitions on a chalkboard.
